benefit_assessment_plain_language: M4p does not make Flye faster. Its CUDA-side benefit is safety: it proves worker overlap records can survive a typed Flye-side conversion and still match CPU in-memory overlap records before any graph construction path is allowed to consume them.

cuda_overlap_worker.nvcc_version:

Cuda compilation tools, release 13.0, V13.0.88
Build cuda_13.0.r13.0/compiler.36424714_0

claims.allowed: cuFlye can rehydrate validated CUDA overlap worker output into a Flye-side typed vector and prove it matches CPU overlap records before graph mutation.
claims.forbidden: No claim that CUDA overlap output is consumed by Flye graph logic, and no end-to-end GPU Flye speed claim.
